comment: Sometimes "I understand" is the final hiding place.
notes: Use when the video is about recognizing awareness but the comments are intellectualizing.

comment: The one looking for the end of seeking is made of seeking.
A pointer becomes entertainment when nothing is risked.
notes: Use when a teaching clip is being consumed passively.

comment: The path becomes real when it stops giving you identity.
notes: Use when the video critiques spiritual identity.

comment: Look at the one who wants the answer.
notes: Use under Who am I or direct inquiry posts.

comment: Seeking becomes safe when seeing would change your life.
You can use wisdom to avoid truth.
notes: Use when the post names knowledge as avoidance.

comment: You do not need another prompt. You need to see who keeps asking for one.
The first honest sentence usually appears after the beautiful ones fail.
Writing is dangerous when you cannot edit the self that appears.
notes: Use when the content names raw writing.

comment: What you write before you can polish it is closer to prayer.
notes: Use sparingly; can sound mystical if context is weak.

comment: Spiritual ego survives by sounding humble.
If it makes you sound awakened, be suspicious.
The mirror is not impressed by spiritual vocabulary.
The most dangerous mask is the one that sounds healed.
notes: Use under performed vulnerability or therapy-speak critique.

comment: You can turn even authenticity into performance.
The marketplace sells identity to people trying to lose identity.
The guru is easiest to see outside. Harder to see as the voice inside that wants to be special.
notes: Use when comments externalize blame too easily.

comment: Awakening became content. That is the wound.
notes: Use only when the video is already naming the marketplace.

comment: Practice becomes real when it removes performance.
The posture is not the point. The hiding is.
notes: Use under meditation aesthetics or posture discussion.

comment: Silence is brutal because there is nowhere to edit yourself.
The nervous system understands before the mind admits it.
The unconscious prefers images because explanations are too easy to fake.
Sometimes the story knows before the writer does.
notes: Use under story or creative process.

comment: The work is honest when it stops trying to make you look honest.
Children hear the truth underneath the lesson.
The child does not need your performance. They need your presence.
A bedtime story can reveal more than a confession.
Sometimes the dragon in the story is the part of the parent asking to be seen.
notes: Use when story symbolism is directly relevant.
